# UC-17.1.52 · Self-Registration Portal Storm Detection and Bot-Onboarding Defense

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Security, Operations &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*A guest sign-up page on the network — like a sign-in sheet at a hotel — can be abused by automated programs creating fake guest accounts. We watch for many sign-ups from few computers and ring the bell when the pattern looks like a bot.*

---

## Description

Detects self-registration portal storms and bot-driven onboarding patterns — characterised by many user creations from few source IPs or MACs.

## Value

Self-registration portals are a common attack target. Botnets attempt to create thousands of accounts to exhaust IP pools, evade rate-limiting, or onboard malicious devices. Detecting the storm in real-time enables CAPTCHA hardening, IP allow-listing, or temporary portal disable.

## Implementation

Confirm *Self-Registration Portal* category at INFO. Schedule every 5 min, search window `-15m@m to @m`, trigger on count > 0, throttle suppression on `email_domain` for 30 min.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Script 110 self-registrations from 2 source IPs. UC-17.1.52 fires with `suspicious=likely_bot` for the `gmail.com` domain.

### Negative scenario

Run 10 organic self-registrations from various IPs. UC-17.1.52 returns `suspicious=normal`.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- ISE Self-Registration portal enabled.
- `event_calendar.csv` for context.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection
Enable *Self-Registration Portal* category at INFO.

### Step 2 — Create the search
Search in `spl` field. Schedule every 5 min.

### Step 3 — Validate
Provoke as above.

### Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard "ISE — Self-Reg Storm":
- Storm signal tile.
- Geographic distribution.
- Top-N domains.

Alerting: PagerDuty high on `likely_bot`.

### Step 5 — Troubleshooting
- **CAPTCHA still bypassed** — Tune CAPTCHA settings on the portal.
- **Storm persists after IP block** — Attack uses VPN; block by user-agent or behavioural pattern.

## SPL

```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (86100, 86101, 86102, 86104, 86110)
| stats count, dc(guest_user) as unique_users, dc(client_ip) as unique_ips, dc(mac_address) as unique_macs by email_domain
| eval suspicious = case(
    unique_users > 100 AND unique_ips < 5, "likely_bot",
    unique_users > 50 AND unique_macs/unique_users < 0.5, "likely_bot",
    count > 500, "possible_storm",
    true(), "normal")
| where suspicious != "normal"
```

## Visualization

(1) Single value: storm signal (red if `suspicious != normal`). (2) Map: client_ip geographic distribution. (3) Top-N: domains.

## Known False Positives

- **Large event** — A legitimate conference may produce 500+ self-registrations. Cross-reference event calendar.
- **Captive portal landing-page test** — QA testing of the captive portal can produce burst traffic; tag and exclude.

## References

- [Cisco ISE 3.4 — Guest Self-Registration](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/security/ise/3-4/admin_guide/b_ise_admin_3_4/m_guest.html)
